# Nodal LMP Price Map

> Interactive map of live locational marginal prices (LMP) at every pricing node across major US ISOs: PJM, CAISO, NYISO, MISO, ERCOT, SPP, and ISO-NE. Updates every 60 seconds.

This tool visualizes wholesale electricity prices at the nodal level, in real time. It is one of the free, open-source tools built by [Kardashev Labs](https://kardashevlabs.org).

## What is LMP?

Locational marginal price (LMP) is the real-time cost of electricity at a specific point (node) on the grid. Each ISO/RTO computes it as the sum of three components:

- **Energy**: the marginal cost of generating the next MW of electricity
- **Congestion**: the added cost from transmission constraints between the node and the reference bus
- **Loss**: the cost attributable to physical transmission losses

LMPs vary node to node and update continuously as grid conditions change, which is why prices can differ sharply between two nearby locations during periods of congestion.

## Coverage

7 major US ISO/RTOs: PJM, CAISO, NYISO, MISO, ERCOT, SPP, and ISO-NE. Both real-time (RT) and day-ahead (DA) markets are available per ISO.

## Data sources

Each ISO/RTO's public real-time and day-ahead wholesale electricity market feeds, served via the kardashev-data platform.

## Features

- Live nodal map, color-coded by price, refreshed every 60 seconds
- Click any node to inspect its current price (energy/congestion/loss breakdown) and 14-day price history
- Toggle between real-time (RT) and day-ahead (DA) markets

## More

Part of [Kardashev Labs](https://kardashevlabs.org), an open-source organization building free tools for US energy grid intelligence. See the main [llms.txt](https://kardashevlabs.org/llms.txt) for the full list of tools. Source code: https://github.com/kardashev-lab.
